prompt:
Provisioning keeps mis-diagnosing 'is the right build of a tool on this box' because everyone checks with a different incantation and half of them answer from the shell's memory instead of from disk. I want one boring, honest resolver: `pathfind.sh`, repo root, bash 5, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`.

Usage: `pathfind.sh [-a] <name>` — resolve <name> against $PATH the way execution would, by walking the entries YOURSELF with test operators. Hard requirement: no `type`, no `command -v`, no `hash`, no calling `which`. Two reasons: (1) those answer from the calling shell's hash table when it has an entry, and our provisioning shell's table is routinely out of date after installs; (2) the provisioning tool runs this resolver with `. pathfind.sh` inside a throwaway subshell after doing `set -- <args>`, so it executes in a shell where that stale table is live — the harness deliberately preloads an out-of-date hash entry and your answer must come from $PATH anyway. Exiting is fine (the subshell is disposable), but every lookup decision must come from your own $PATH loop. Related: use builtins only — no external commands at all — since the tool gets pointed at awkward-to-empty PATH values where externals wouldn't resolve.

Resolution rules:
- Split $PATH on ':'. An EMPTY segment (leading colon, trailing colon, or '::') means the current directory, searched at exactly that position; print a match there as `./<name>`. A zero-length $PATH is ONE empty segment (current dir only), and an unset PATH behaves the same as a zero-length one.
- A candidate `<dir>/<name>` counts only if it is a regular file with execute permission. Skip — silently — directories that happen to be named like the command, non-executable regular files, and anything missing.
- Default: print the FIRST match and exit 0. With `-a`: print EVERY match, one per line, in PATH order, without deduplicating repeated directories, and exit 0 if there was at least one.
- No match: print `pathfind.sh: <name>: not found in PATH` to stderr, nothing to stdout, exit 1.

Errors (all exit 64): missing name, more than one name, an option that isn't `-a`, or an empty-string name -> `usage: pathfind.sh [-a] <name>` on stderr. A name containing '/' -> `pathfind.sh: name must not contain '/'` on stderr.

`bash test_pathfind.sh` from the workspace root is the acceptance gate; it builds its own fixture bin dirs (executable, non-executable, directory-named-like-the-command, the lot). Don't modify the test file.
